Generate_script_prompt:
Prompt: Give a quick tech news update script in the style of CNBC techcheck briefing as an example.
        Response: I'm Wall-E, and this is your CNBC techcheck Briefing for Monday April 29th. Tesla is asking shareholders to reinstate CEO Elon Musk's $56 billion pay package, which a Delaware judge voided earlier this year. The judge ruled that the record-setting compensation deal was, quote, deeply flawed. Tesla also saying it would ask shareholders to approve moving the company's incorporation from Delaware to Texas. The company has hired a proxy solicitor and plans to spend millions of dollars to help secure votes for the two proposals. Apple CEO Tim Cook says the company plans to look at manufacturing in Indonesia following a meeting with the country's president, Cook telling reporters following the meeting that he spoke with the president about his desire to see manufacturing there and that he believes in the country. The comments come as Apple is pushed to diversify its supply chain with more manufacturing outside of China in countries such as Vietnam and India. Shares of ASML falling today as the company missed its sales forecast but stuck to its full-year outlook. Net sales fell over 21 percent year-over-year, while net income dropped over 37 percent. ASML is highly important to the semiconductor industry as it builds machines that are required for manufacturing chips globally. Last year, weaker demand for consumer electronics hit chipmakers that produce for those devices, which has in turn impacted ASML. That's all for today. We'll see you back here tomorrow.

Response:
Script:
i'm wall-e, and this is your ai briefing for monday, april 20th. microsoft announced plans to integrate openai's advanced language models into its office suite, enhancing productivity tools such as word and excel. this move aims to create more intuitive and efficient platform features, using ai to streamline tasks and boost user experience.

meanwhile, google is upping its investments in renewable energy, committing an additional $2 billion to solar and wind projects. this investment aligns with google's goal of operating on carbon-free energy across its global data centers by 2030, further cementing its position as a leader in sustainable tech practices.

in other news, amazon is set to launch satellite internet services through its project kuiper by the end of the year. competing with spacex’s starlink, amazon plans to deploy a constellation of over 3,000 satellites, aiming to provide global internet coverage, especially in remote and underserved areas.

finally, uber shares are on the rise after the ride-hailing giant reported exceeding forecasts for its active users in the first quarter of 2023, highlighting a strong rebound in post-pandemic travel demand.

that's all for today. we'll see you back here tomorrow
Polished Script:
"i'm wall-e, welcoming you to today's tech briefing for monday, april 20th.

microsoft has unveiled plans to incorporate openai's advanced language models into its office suite. this integration is set to enhance tools like word and excel, aiming to create more intuitive and efficient features. by leveraging ai, microsoft intends to streamline tasks and elevate the user experience.

turning to renewables, google is making a significant push by investing an additional $2 billion into solar and wind projects. this move is part of google's broader strategy to fully operate on carbon-free energy across its global data centers by 2030, reinforcing its status as a leader in sustainable technology practices.

on the connectivity front, amazon is gearing up to launch its satellite internet services via project kuiper by the end of the year. competing with spacex’s starlink, amazon plans to deploy over 3,000 satellites, with the goal of providing global internet coverage, particularly to remote and underserved regions.

lastly, uber is seeing a rise in its shares after surpassing forecasts for active users in the first quarter of 2023. this milestone highlights a robust demand for travel services as we move beyond the pandemic era.

that's your tech update for today. we'll see you back here tomorrow."
Podcast Title: EP-521 Microsoft's Ai-powered Office Suite 🌟, Google's $2b Renewable Push ☀️, Amazon's Satellite Internet Launch 🚀
Podcast Description:
welcome to wall-e's tech briefing for monday, april 20th! explore the latest updates in the tech world:

  • microsoft's ai integration: plans to enhance its office suite by incorporating openai's advanced language models, aiming for more intuitive and efficient features in word and excel.
  • google's renewable energy investment: a $2 billion investment in solar and wind projects as part of the goal to operate on carbon-free energy across all data centers by 2030.
  • amazon's satellite internet launch: project kuiper aims to provide global internet coverage with over 3,000 satellites, targeting remote and underserved regions, competing with spacex's starlink.
  • uber's rising share prices: surpassed forecasts for active users in q1 2023, indicating strong demand for travel services post-pandemic.

stay tuned for tomorrow's tech updates!
Image Prompt: a unified podcast cover image that seamlessly combines the themes of cutting-edge technology and renewable energy. the scene features a vibrant earth viewed from space during daylight, centered on a lush landscape with wind turbines and solar panels, symbolizing google's renewable energy initiatives. in orbit above the earth, an elegantly designed satellite represents amazon's ambitious satellite internet project, with shimmering beams of data flowing down toward the planet. hovering in the foreground is a sleek laptop displaying an ai interface, with abstract, dynamic digital elements swirling around it, representing microsoft's ai-powered office suite. the composition blends the terrestrial and celestial elements, encapsulating the synergy of technology-driven progress toward a sustainable future
